reports.summary: Game concept locked: Penny Panic is a 2-player same-device split-screen competitive tap game where players race to collect 10 pennies while shooting bugs in an infinite void.
changes: Game concept defined by user: 2 players, 10 pennies, bug shooting, the void
Full game design document written — game.md populated with all entity definitions, systems, screen flow, audio design, visual design, and performance targets
4-milestone implementation plan written and tracked in memory/future_milestones.md
Vite project initialized on port 5212
Folder structure and module contracts defined: state.js, input.js, renderer.js, entities/, systems/, screens/, audio/, fx/
The Void aesthetic established as a design principle: pure #000000 background, no decorative elements, maximum contrast for game objects
Penny spawn distribution strategy: 40% P1 half / 40% P2 half / 20% center seam (contested territory)
Bug difficulty curve designed: wave 1 = 80px/s, caps at 180px/s by wave 6, escalating every 20 seconds
Audio design planned: zero audio files, all sounds via Web Audio API OscillatorNode + BufferSourceNode
Multitouch architecture planned: input.js processes TouchList, collision.js resolves all touches before advancing entity state
Best-of-3 round system planned for M4 with native share sheet integration
bugs: No bugs — pre-implementation report. See milestone reports for runtime bugs.
suggestions: Center seam penny spawns (20% of total) create natural flashpoints — consider whether the seam zone should pulse visually to signal a contested penny is available
P2's rotated 180° HUD is elegant but may cause disorientation on first play — consider a brief tutorial overlay on first launch
Bug despawn-without-penalty if both players miss (vs guaranteed -1 pt) could reduce feel-bad moments in early rounds
A 3-second countdown before each round starts would give players time to position their fingers
Consider a 'practice mode' where bugs are disabled so new players can learn penny mechanics first
recommendations: Build state.js first — every other module depends on it. Define the full shape of gameState before writing any entity or system code.
Get multitouch working correctly in input.js before implementing any hit detection — it's the most fragile part of M1 and wrong assumptions compound across all systems
Use a fixed-timestep game loop from the start (accumulator pattern) — variable delta can cause physics drift and makes bug speed curves non-deterministic
Pre-allocate the particle pool in M1 even if particles aren't used yet — retrofitting a pool into an allocating system is harder than starting with one
Test on a real iPhone before M1 is 'done' — mobile Safari's touch coordinate model has subtle differences from desktop mouse emulation that matter for split-screen

For the void aesthetic: resist the temptation to add any background element. The emptiness is load-bearing. If the game 'feels empty' the solution is better game feel (particles, audio, screen flash), not visual noise.
ideas: CHAOS MODE: penny count doubles to 20, bugs spawn 3× faster — unlock after winning a best-of-3
SUDDEN DEATH round: first player to miss a bug loses — triggered if series reaches 2-2 (for a best-of-5 expansion)
BUG VARIANTS: rare golden bug worth +5 pts, fast silver bug that weaves, slow fat bug that takes 2 taps
PENNY TYPES: rare 'wheat penny' worth 3 pts, 'silver penny' worth 2 pts — same tap mechanic, different glow color
VOID EVENTS: rare cosmetic — a penny falls impossibly slow, a bug spirals in, the void 'breathes' (barely perceptible scale pulse on all objects simultaneously)
REMATCH BUTTON with animated score history comparison across sessions (localStorage)
reports.notes: The game's hook is the tension between two simultaneous activities: racing your opponent for pennies (tapping the same side of the screen as them) while also defending your own half from bugs. The void aesthetic is not laziness or minimalism for its own sake — it maximizes the cognitive load of the game objects by giving them zero competition for visual attention. Every design decision should serve that tension. The name 'Penny Panic' is a working title — something weirder and more specific to the void theme might emerge during implementation.

reports.summary: Fully playable round: two players compete for 10 pennies on a split-screen canvas with bug entities, score tracking, particle effects, screen flashes, and round end screen.
changes: Vite project initialized on port 5212, vanilla JS (no TypeScript)
index.html: single canvas element, full-viewport CSS, portrait meta, touch-action:none
src/constants.js: all magic numbers defined — canvas dims, penny/bug specs, colors, timing
src/state.js: single gameState object as source of truth; freshRoundState(), resetRound(), announceScore()
src/input.js: touchstart + mousedown listeners, multi-touch via changedTouches, canvas coordinate conversion
src/renderer.js: DPR-aware canvas, clear-to-void each frame, layered draw fn registration by priority, dynamic seam line
src/entities/penny.js: Penny class with drop-in animation (ease-out cubic), alpha fade on lifetime expiry, copper+highlight draw, circular hit test
src/entities/bug.js: Bug class with direction vector to target half center, sinusoidal wobble, wing flicker (8-frame alternate), canvas-drawn body/legs/antennae, miss detection at canvas boundary
src/entities/particle.js: pre-allocated pool of 128 particles, spawnBurst(), gravity, alpha fade
src/systems/spawner.js: 10 pennies/round, 1.2s intervals, max 3 on screen, 40/40/20 zone distribution, min-distance check
src/systems/difficulty.js: bug wave escalation — speed 80→180px/s, interval 5s→2s, wave 3+ side edge spawns
src/systems/collision.js: bugs resolved before pennies, per-touch entity resolution, no ghost hits
src/systems/scoring.js: claimPenny, shootBug, missBug, isRoundOver
src/fx/flash.js: half-screen or full-screen color flash with linear alpha fade, multiple flashes queue correctly
src/screens/title.js: PENNY PANIC title both halves (P2 rotated 180°), pulsing center penny, tap to start
src/screens/game.js: full tick loop, penny+bug update, collision resolution, particle spawning on hits, round-over detection
src/screens/roundEnd.js: winner banner, side-by-side scores, bugs shot per player, tap-to-continue with 1.2s debounce
src/main.js: RAF game loop with 50ms dt cap, DPR-aware canvas, screen.orientation.lock, screen state machine
All coordinates dynamic (canvasH/2 seam) — works on any viewport, not hardcoded to 393×852
bugs: Penny counter dots show penniesResolved which counts both claimed AND despawned pennies — could be confusing if pennies expire without being collected (appears as claimed)
Bug wobble uses perpendicular drift accumulation per frame rather than absolute position, causing slight path drift over time — not noticeable in practice but technically incorrect
Title screen pennyPulseAge increments by hardcoded 16ms per draw call rather than using actual dt — will drift at non-60fps
P2 HUD label says 'P2' but the penny dots are shown in P1's rotated view — both halves show the same penniesResolved count which is correct, but visually redundant
Round end 'tap to continue' restarts a full new round (resets round state) — no best-of-3 series tracking yet (M4 feature)
suggestions: Add a 3-2-1 countdown before pennies start spawning — gives players time to get their thumbs in position
Penny despawn should probably NOT count as 'resolved' for scoring purposes — currently a penny timing out ends the round faster than expected
Consider showing which half a newly spawned penny belongs to with a brief flash at the seam — center seam pennies are natural conflict zones
The round end score display would benefit from a brief winner celebration animation before 'tap to continue' appears
Bug hit detection should prioritize bugs closest to the player boundary — bugs almost at the wall are more urgent
recommendations: Before M2, separate penny despawn tracking from penniesResolved — despawned pennies should not advance the round-end counter, only claimed pennies should. Otherwise rounds can end before all pennies were competed for.
Fix title screen pulse to use actual dt passed through from main loop rather than hardcoded 16ms increment
Consider adding a brief 'invincibility' window after a bug miss so one bug doesn't chain-penalty into multiple deductions
ideas: Visual indicator when a penny is about to expire (flash more urgently as lifetime approaches 8s)
Sound design for M3: the void should be near-silent except for game events — no ambient music, just sharp precise audio cues
reports.notes: M1 is fully playable. The void aesthetic is working — pure black background, copper pennies, white bugs, everything immediately readable. The split-screen layout with P2 rotated 180° works well for same-device multiplayer. Main architectural win: all seam calculations are dynamic (canvasH/2) so the game works correctly on any screen size. Main outstanding issue before M2: the penny despawn/resolve tracking logic should be tightened up.

reports.summary: Audio system live (Web Audio API, zero files), 3-2-1 countdown before each round, penny expiry flicker, score scale animation on change, mute toggle with localStorage persistence, fixed penny despawn round-end logic.
changes: src/audio/synth.js: Web Audio API synthesizer — playCoin (C5→E5 glide), playBug (white noise), playMiss (sawtooth thud), playWin (4-note arpeggio), playBeep (countdown), playPennyAppear
AudioContext lazy-initialized on first touch (iOS Safari gate), setMuted() for global mute
3-2-1 countdown before each round — gameplay blocked during countdown, beep per number, high beep on GO
Penny expiry flicker: slow flicker at 6s, fast flicker at 7s (300ms/150ms), draws urgency without obscuring
Score number animates on change: scale 1.35× → 1.0× over 200ms, both players tracked independently
Mute toggle button in top-right corner of each player's half (canvas-drawn speaker/X icon, ~44px target)
Mute persisted to localStorage key 'pp_muted', restored on page load

Fixed penny despawn round-end bug: penniesResolved now counts only claimed pennies (p.owner !== null), not expired ones. Round ends when all 10 spawned AND no active pennies remain — expired pennies no longer prematurely end rounds
Small amber burst on penny expiry to indicate it vanished (not claimed)
bugs: Countdown is per-round but not shown on round-end screen — when tap-to-continue triggers a new round, the countdown appears but briefly overlaps with the transition
Mute button is drawn in both P1 and P2 HUD (each rotated half) — tapping either corner works, but visually appears in both halves which could be confusing
Score animation scale accumulates if score changes rapidly (back-to-back events reset timer before animation completes) — visually fine but mathematically incorrect
playPennyAppear not wired up — penny spawn has no sound yet
Bug wobble path drift still present (perpendicular accumulated per frame) — minor
suggestions: Countdown 'GO!' text could flash green rather than white for more excitement
A brief visual indicator on the seam when a contested (center-zone) penny spawns would add tension
Bug miss sound is quite loud relative to coin collect — consider normalizing gain levels across all sounds
Round end should show how many pennies each player collected vs expired — gives more information
recommendations: Wire playPennyAppear to spawner.js spawn event before M3
Evaluate if the countdown 3s delay is too long for repeat play — consider reducing to 2s after first round
Consider adding a brief 'safe window' after round start before bugs spawn (give players time to orient)
ideas: Combo system: collect 3 pennies in a row without a bug miss = bonus multiplier
Bug sounds could vary by bug type if bug variants are added in a future milestone
reports.notes: The audio system works well. The void-plus-audio combination is exactly right — silence except for precise event sounds makes every collect feel satisfying. The countdown is essential for same-device multiplayer (players need a moment to get their thumbs positioned). The penny flicker is a small detail that reads immediately in play.

reports.summary: Full best-of-3 match flow: round wins are tracked, Match End screen shows series winner and per-round breakdown, series dots display on round end screen.
changes: Added SCREENS.MATCH_END to state.js (was already declared, now actually wired)
Added roundHistory array to gameState — stores {scores:[p1,p2]} per completed round
resetMatch() now clears roundHistory on new match
enterRoundEnd() now records round result and awards roundWins before showing screen
handleRoundEndInput() checks roundWins — if either player has 2 wins, goes to MATCH_END; otherwise increments currentRound and continues
Created src/screens/matchEnd.js — draws match winner, per-round score breakdown, win dots, tap-to-play-again
matchEnd draws both P1 and P2 halves (P2 rotated 180°) so both players can read the result
Round end screen now shows series dots (● ○) reflecting each player's current round wins
main.js wired to handle MATCH_END screen in game loop — enterMatchEnd, tickMatchEnd, handleMatchEndInput
bugs: If a round ends in a tie, no roundWin is awarded — series could theoretically never reach 2 wins (edge case, unlikely in practice)
Match end tap target is whole screen — players could accidentally restart by grazing the screen
suggestions: Add a brief celebration animation on the winning half at match end (particle burst from score)
Show round number in game HUD so players know which round they're in mid-game
recommendations: Consider adding chaosMode unlock trigger at match end (M11 feature) — the match end screen is the right place for this prompt
roundHistory could accumulate across matches if resetMatch isn't called correctly — verify the flow from title → game → round_end → match_end → title
ideas: Show 'ROUND X of 3' on countdown screen for context
reports.notes: The best-of-3 flow works correctly. Rounds now advance through a proper match arc. The series dots on round end are a clear communication of match state.

reports.summary: Share result button on Match End screen and ambient void depth particle layer (60 semi-transparent drifting dots at draw layer 0).
changes: src/fx/voidDepth.js: 60 particles, r=0.8-2px, rgba(255,255,255,0.035), slow drift (8-12px/s), wrap at canvas edges, registered at draw layer 0 via registerLayer
initVoidDepth() called from main.js at startup before enterTitle()
Share button rendered on Match End screen in each player's half near the dots
getShareText() builds share string: 'Penny Panic: P1 N – P2 N (P? wins the match). radgh.github.io/RSG-Demos/game12/'
handleMatchEndInput() checks share button hit area before restarting — uses navigator.share() with clipboard fallback
Animated HTML report generated at public/demo-assets/report-m5.html covering M1-M5 with scroll-reveal, void particle canvas, milestone grid, architecture overview
bugs: Void particles initialize at first draw call — if canvas resizes before first draw, particles may cluster
Share hit area uses approximate Y coordinates — may miss on extreme aspect ratios
suggestions: Add 3-5 slightly brighter copper floaters (0.08 opacity) among the void depth particles for depth layering
Share text could include emoji for quick scanning: '🪙 Penny Panic'
recommendations: Before M6: refactor scoring.js to support per-entity point values — current fixed constants won't work for gold/wheat variants
ideas: Void depth particles could react subtly to game events — briefly brightening on penny collect
reports.notes: Void depth effect is barely visible by design (0.035 opacity). It adds subperceptual motion to the otherwise static black background. The game loop runs cleanly at layer 0.

reports.summary: Three bug types: normal (75%), gold (15%, +5 pts, 1.5× speed), fast silver (10%, +2 pts, 2× speed, smaller). Each type has distinct visuals.
changes: Bug class constructor now accepts bugType parameter ('normal'|'gold'|'fast')
Gold bug: radius unchanged, golden body color #f5d06e, copper legs #c8922a, gold highlight ring drawn around body, speed 1.5× base
Fast bug: radius 0.75× base, dimmer white body (rgba 0.7 opacity), speed 2× base, all geometry scaled proportionally
rollBugType() in difficulty.js: 15% gold, 10% fast, 75% normal
getBugPoints(bug) in scoring.js: gold=5, fast=2, normal=2
shootBug() uses getBugPoints() instead of constant BUG_KILL_POINTS
bugs: Fast bugs are harder to tap due to smaller radius — combined with 2× speed, may be frustrating on first encounter
Bug variant type is not shown in any HUD tooltip — players discover by tapping
suggestions: Brief text popup '+5!' at gold bug kill location (part of M8 combo system could handle this)
Fast bug could leave a brief motion trail (3 ghost circles at 20% opacity)
ideas: Boss bug: huge slow bug worth +10 pts, takes 2 taps to kill
reports.notes: Gold bugs at 15% spawn rate and +5 pts create meaningful upside for aggressive play. Fast bugs at 2× speed are a real threat.

reports.summary: Three penny types: normal (75%, +1 pt), silver (15%, +2 pts, silver color), wheat (10%, +3 pts, larger, dark copper).
changes: Penny class constructor accepts pennyType ('normal'|'wheat'|'silver')
Wheat penny: radius 1.2× base, body color #8B4513, highlight rgba(200,120,60,0.35)
Silver penny: radius unchanged, body color #C0C0C0, highlight rgba(240,240,255,0.4)
rollPennyType() in spawner.js: 10% wheat, 15% silver, 75% normal
getPennyPoints(penny) in scoring.js: wheat=3, silver=2, normal=1
claimPenny() uses getPennyPoints() for variable point awards
bugs: Wheat penny slightly larger radius makes it easier to tap — could be considered overpowered given higher value
Penny type not shown in any point popup — players don't know why they got 3 pts until they learn the visual distinction
suggestions: Brief '+3!' text burst at wheat penny claim location
Consider adding a subtle sparkle or secondary glow ring for silver/wheat pennies to help new players identify them
reports.notes: Penny variety adds strategic depth. Wheat pennies visible size difference makes them feel premium. Silver vs normal is subtler and rewards attentive players.

reports.summary: Consecutive penny claims build a combo counter per player — x3+ awards 3pts, breaks on bug miss or 4s inactivity.
changes: comboCount[2] and comboTimer[2] added to freshRoundState() in state.js
tickCombos(dt) in scoring.js advances combo timers, resets combo at 4000ms
breakCombo(playerIdx) called on bug miss in game.js tickGame
claimPenny() increments comboCount, resets timer, awards 3pts if combo>=3
Burst color changes: normal=gold #f5d06e, x2=orange #ff8822, x3+=red #ff4444; burst count 16 at x3+
Combo counter displayed in HUD when combo>=2: 'x2' in orange, 'x3+' in red, positioned right of score
bugs: Combo counter always shows combo at time of draw, not smoothly animated — it jumps in/out
Wheat penny at 3pt overrides combo bonus at 3pt — effectively no benefit for wheat+combo (same 3pts)
suggestions: Combo counter could pulse/scale briefly when it increments
Wheat penny combo could award 4pts for clarity of value
reports.notes: Combo system adds pace variance and rewards consistent play. The 4s timeout is forgiving enough to survive one bad spawn gap.

reports.summary: Penny pulse animation, center seam amber pulse on contested spawns, subtle vignette overlay, bug death × text that floats up and fades.
changes: Penny.draw(): pulse = 1 + 0.03 * sin(Date.now/1200 * 2π) — radius scales ±3% on 1.2s sine wave
gameState.seamPulseTimer added — spawner.js sets it to 400ms on center-zone penny spawn
renderer.js drawSeam uses seamPulseTimer to interpolate seam color from dim white to amber #c8922a
Vignette: radial gradient overlay (transparent center → rgba 0,0,0,0.15 at edges) drawn after all layers
Bug death text: deathTexts array in round state, × drawn at bug kill position, floats up 10px, fades over 300ms
bugs: Death texts are stored in round.deathTexts but the variable name `dt` shadows the outer `dt` parameter in the tick loop — works correctly but is confusing naming
Vignette redraws every frame via createRadialGradient — could be cached but not a perf issue at this scale
suggestions: Penny pulse phase could be offset per-penny (using penny.id as offset seed) so all pennies don't pulse in unison
Seam amber pulse could show 'CONTESTED' text for 1s (deferred to M14)
reports.notes: The vignette is extremely subtle at 0.15 opacity — exactly right for the void aesthetic. The seam amber pulse communicates contested territory without distracting from gameplay.
